headline: Mongolia FIRST BASELINE — UN HCHR Volker Türk visited May 10-11 (first UN Human Rights Chief visit in 26 years). Türk praised Mongolia's recent adoption of the region's first law protecting human rights defenders. Highlighted as model for nuclear-weapons-free zone status AND death-penalty abolition. Concerns: corruption ('biggest bottleneck to development' per civil society); climate change (dzuds, floods, droughts); digital technology impact on youth and politics. UN Independent Expert on SOGI (Graeme Reid) country visit scheduled May 18-29. Published composite: 48.4 Functional. Assessment: 48.4 confirmed at first baseline. Pre-positive-event scoring; positive dimensional pressure +1.5 on AWR/BND from human-rights-defenders law + UN engagement (sub-threshold, carry-forward per v1.2). Confirm at 48.4.

internal_reconstructed_dimensions_with_c.carry_forward_disposition: Per v1.2, +1.3 dimensional pressure preserved. SOGI visit (May 18-29) could add EQU dimensional evidence; combined with current pressure, May 21+ reassessment could cross threshold.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3 unchanged at published level. Internal +0.1: UN HCHR engagement opens awareness channels.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 unchanged. SOGI visit (May 18-29) will generate EQU evidence in next cycle.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3 unchanged at published level. Internal +0.2: human-rights-defenders law adoption is a B-dimension positive — first such law in region.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3 unchanged. Türk visit creates accountability infrastructure but no AB-dimension shift yet.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3 unchanged. Corruption flagged as structural bottleneck (offsetting human-rights-defenders positive).
scoring_rationale.INT: 3 unchanged at published level. Internal +0.2: hosted first UN HCHR visit in 26 years — costly-trust-building action.

Mongolia highlighted as model for nuclear-weapons-free zone status AND death-penalty abolition.
Concerns raised: corruption ('biggest bottleneck to development'); climate change (dzuds, floods, droughts); digital technology impact on youth.
UN Independent Expert on SOGI (Graeme Reid) country visit May 18-29 — EQU evidence generation forthcoming.

math_check.validator_check: First baseline confirmation. +1.3 internal carry-forward pressure logged.

negativeSignals: Corruption flagged by civil society as 'biggest bottleneck to development'; climate change (dzuds, floods, droughts) structural drag; digital-rights concerns for youth.
positiveSignals: First UN HCHR visit in 26 years; human-rights-defenders law (regional first); death-penalty abolition; nuclear-weapons-free zone; SOGI visit scheduled May 18-29.
sector_context: East Asia / democratic-transition cluster. Mongolia's 48.4 placement is upper Functional, near Established band. Positive carry-forward + SOGI visit could trigger upgrade in June rotation.
open_actions_carried: Forward signal: SOGI visit May 18-29 — re-assessment ~June 5
methodology_consistency_check: First baseline at published score; carry-forward pressure preserved. Methodologically consistent with v1.2.
